svg.title: Lab architecture v5 with diagnose query fanning out to all backends
svg.desc: Same as before, but the diagnose RCA query now fans out to all three backends (Dash0, Dynatrace, Datadog) over PromQL, mirroring the collector ingest fan-out, reflecting the backend-agnostic query design.
svg.text: Signal sources → one pipeline → many backends → AI diagnosis
AWS EKS CLUSTER · (≈ your data-center fabric)
≈ app agent + inline tap + NetFlow exporter, in-kernel
cross-node link — fault here (≈ inter-switch path)
Cilium CNI enforces policy (≈ ACL / firewall rule)
The loop: users call the gateway → signals generated in-kernel → one pipeline → fanned out to every backend.
/diagnose can query any of those backends back over the exact incident window and calls llm-svc for the root cause.
Every Bedrock call (recommendations and diagnose alike) emits a gen_ai.* span — the AI is observed by the system it runs.
Five correlated fault signals: hubble_drop_total (POLICY_DENY) · obi.network.flow.bytes · obi.stat.tcp.failed.connections ·
beyla.network.flow.bytes · dash0.spans. The network layer shows the cause; the app layer shows only the silence.
